name: First Bank Of Central Ohio
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank serving central Ohio with personal and business banking, lending, and specialty services for real estate, medical, and dental practices.
description_long:
First Bank of Central Ohio is a community-focused FDIC-insured bank operating in the Columbus, Ohio area with two primary locations in Worthington and Upper Arlington. The bank emphasizes personalized service and local decision-making, positioning itself as an alternative to larger national banks while maintaining federal deposit insurance backing.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of personal banking services including checking, savings accounts, certificates of deposit, and digital payment tools like Zelle and mobile wallet integration. For consumers, lending options include home mortgages and home equity lines of credit. On the business side, FBCO provides business checking and savings accounts, business credit cards, business lines of credit, commercial installment loans, SBA loans, and specialized commercial real estate financing including land acquisition and development loans.

First Bank of Central Ohio distinguishes itself through industry-specific expertise, with dedicated specialty services for property management companies, commercial real estate investors, and medical and dental practices. The bank promotes "personal" business banking and community involvement, contrasting itself with larger institutional competitors. They offer modern digital conveniences like external fund transfers at no cost, Apple Pay/Samsung Pay/Google Pay integration, and online and mobile banking platforms.

As a regional community bank, FBCO serves a limited geographic footprint (only two locations in central Ohio) and may have less extensive branch access than national competitors. The website provides limited information about account fee structures, specific interest rates, and minimum balance requirements, which are critical factors for consumers evaluating banking options. For those in the central Ohio region seeking relationship-based banking with specialized business services, this bank offers viable options.
services: Commercial land acquisition and development financing
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of the U.S. Government
No-cost external transfers between FBCO and other financial institutions
Mobile wallet support (Apple Pay, Samsung Pay, Google Pay) for secure contactless payments
Specialized lending expertise for medical/dental practices, property management, and commercial real estate
Small Business Administration (SBA) loan programs available for qualifying businesses
Dedicated business banking focus with commitment to community lending
Multiple lending options including commercial real estate financing and land development loans
cons: Limited geographic footprint with only two physical branch locations in central Ohio
No published information on checking/savings account fees, monthly maintenance charges, or minimum balance requirements
Interest rates and CD yields not displayed on website, requiring direct contact to compare
Limited service area may inconvenience customers relocating outside central Ohio
No mention of checking account overdraft protection or fee structures
best_for: Small business owners and entrepreneurs in central Ohio seeking relationship-based commercial banking
Medical and dental practice owners needing specialized financial services and healthcare industry expertise
Commercial real estate investors and property management companies in the central Ohio region
Central Ohio residents seeking FDIC-insured personal banking with community bank service model
diagnosis: First Bank of Central Ohio is best for small business owners, real estate investors, and medical/dental professionals in the central Ohio area who prioritize personalized service and industry expertise over branch convenience. The main caveat is the very limited geographic footprint (two locations only) and lack of publicly available fee and rate information, requiring direct contact to evaluate actual banking costs.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
